title: Sundesert Nuclear Power Plant units 1-2, construction : environmental impact statement.

entities.quote: on the basis of the analysis and evaluation set forth in this Statement, after weighing the environmental, economic, technical, and other benefits of Sundesert Nuclear Plant Units 1 and 2 against environmental and other costs and considering available alternatives, it is concluded that the action called for under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 (NEPA) and 10 CFR Part 51 is the issuance of construction permits for the plant subject to the following conditions for the protection of the environment
entities.stance_basis: NRC concludes construction permits should be issued subject to conditions

entities.quote: This Environmental Statement was prepared by the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission, Office of Nuclear Reactor Regulation, and the U.S. Department of the Interior, Bureau of Land Management and Bureau of Reclamation.
entities.stance_basis: Co-prepared EIS recommending issuance of construction permits

entities.entity: San Diego Gas and Electric Company
entities.quote: an application, with an accompanying Environmental Report (ER), was filed by San Diego Gas and Electric Company (SDG&E) and accepted for docketing on April 13, 1977.
entities.stance_basis: Filed application for construction permits for the Sundesert Nuclear Plant

entities.quote: Other entities planning to participate with SDG&E in the proposed plant are: the City of Anaheim (Anaheim), the City of Burbank (Burbank), the City of Glendale (Glendale), the City of Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP), the City of Pasadena (Pasadena), the City of Riverside (Riverside), and the California Department of Water Resources (CDWR).
entities.stance_basis: Planning to participate as co-owner of the proposed plant

entities.quote: Other entities planning to participate with SDG&E in the proposed plant are: the City of Anaheim (Anaheim), the City of Burbank (Burbank), the City of Glendale (Glendale), the City of Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP), the City of Pasadena (Pasadena), the City of Riverside (Riverside), and the California Department of Water Resources (CDWR).
entities.stance_basis: Planning to participate as co-owner of the proposed plant

entities.stance_basis: Planning to participate as co-owner of the proposed plant

entities.stance_basis: Planning to participate as co-owner of the proposed plant

entities.stance_basis: Planning to participate as co-owner of the proposed plant

entities.stance_basis: Planning to participate as co-owner of the proposed plant

entities.entity: California Energy Resources Conservation and Development Commission
entities.quote: The decision states, "The Notice of Intent is approved, subject to the conditions and modifications set forth in Appendix A, for one unit of the proposed multiple unit facility on the basis of there being one acceptable site capable of use for two units of the type proposed for the Sundesert Nuclear Project."
entities.stance_basis: Approved Notice of Intent for one unit subject to conditions and modifications

entities.quote: The BLM and BuRec of DOI have participated extensively in the preparation of those sections of the DES relevant to land exchange, transmission lines, and water supply.
entities.stance_basis: Participated extensively in DES preparation supporting the project review

entities.quote: The BLM and BuRec of DOI have participated extensively in the preparation of those sections of the DES relevant to land exchange, transmission lines, and water supply.
entities.stance_basis: Participated extensively in DES preparation; granted tentative approval for water diversion

entities.quote: This environmental statement was prepared by the Division of Site Safety and Environmental Analysis, Office of Nuclear Reactor Regulation, U. S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (the staff) and the U. S. Department of the Interior in cooperation with the U. S. Army Corps of Engineers
entities.stance_basis: Cooperating agency providing input under Memorandum of Understanding, no explicit position stated

entities.quote: This Draft Environmental Statement is being made available to the public, to the EPA, and to other specified agencies in January 1978.
entities.stance_basis: Listed as reviewing agency; has general Memorandum of Understanding with NRC but no position stated

entities.role: water rights holder / party to Seven-Party Agreement
entities.quote: SDG&E solicited and was granted tentative approvals from the seven parties and the BuRec.
entities.stance_basis: Granted tentative approval for water diversion to the proposed plant

entities.entity: State Historic Preservation Officers for Arizona and California
entities.quote: The State Historic Preservation Officers for Arizona and California have confirmed that no registered historical sites exist on or within 8 km (5 miles) of the proposed plant site (ER, Sect. 2.6).
entities.stance_basis: confirmed absence of registered historical sites near the proposed plant site

entities.quote: At the suggestion of the State Historic Preservation Officer, NRC has requested the applicant to provide additional information concerning Bradshaw Road, evidence of General Patton's maneuvers, and expected impacts on these features from construction of the Sundesert plant.
entities.stance_basis: suggested NRC request additional information on historic features potentially impacted by construction

entities.quote: They have proposed to NRC that the two areas be nominated for inclusion in the National Register of Historic Places.
entities.stance_basis: proposed preservation of two significant aboriginal workshop sites on the plant site as condition of project proceeding

entities.quote: The California Employment Development Department states that approximately 80% of this work force are Mexican nationals who commute daily from Mexicali and San Luis, Mexico, and 20% are nonlocal U.S. residents living outside the 80-km (50-mile) radius (ER, Sect. 2.1.3.3.2).
entities.stance_basis: provided factual demographic data on agricultural transient workforce composition

entities.quote: The California Employment Development Department states that current (1976) unemployment rates in the valley are running between 9 and 10%.
entities.stance_basis: provided factual unemployment data for the Palo Verde Valley

entities.quote: Riverside County officials state that, in 11 identified apartment developments, there are 416 rental units, ranging from four-unit buildings to a 280-unit complex (ER, Sect. 4.1.6.2.1).
entities.stance_basis: provided factual housing data for the Palo Verde Valley

entities.quote: City officials state that present sewerage facilities will not be capable of handling a large influx of new peoples into the area.
entities.stance_basis: identified infrastructure limitation regarding sewerage capacity relative to population influx from plant construction/operation

entities.quote: The Blythe Chief of Police states that the police force is unusually large for a city the size of Blythe.
entities.stance_basis: provided factual information on police force size relative to community

entities.entity: Riverside County Department of Public Social Services
entities.quote: The Riverside County Department of Public Social Services in Riverside states that the Blythe welfare office handles a case load of approximately 1000 (ER, Sect. 4.1.6.2.5).
entities.stance_basis: provided factual social services caseload data

entities.quote: The Imperial County Welfare Department in El Centro states that the case load in the community of Palo Verde is low due primarily to a large population of retirees living on social security.
entities.stance_basis: provided factual welfare caseload data for Palo Verde community

entities.entity: Yuma Office of the Arizona Department of Economic Security
entities.quote: The Yuma Office of the Arizona Department of Economic Security states that, in the Ehrenberg-Quartzsite-Poston-Parker area, approximately 300 families were receiving food stamps and/or public assistance in the first quarter of 1976 (ER, Sect. 4.1.6.2.5).
entities.stance_basis: provided factual public assistance data for the area

entities.entity: Comprehensive Planning Organization (CPO) of the San Diego Region
entities.quote: This growth will begin to slow down because 85% of its usable land has already been developed, according to CPO.
entities.stance_basis: provided factual land development data for Chula Vista

entities.quote: At the suggestion of the State Historic Preservation Officer, NRC has requested the applicant to provide additional information concerning Bradshaw Road, evidence of General Patton's maneuvers, and expected impacts on these features from construction of the Sundesert plant.
entities.stance_basis: requested additional information on historic features potentially impacted by construction

entities.role: Bureau of Land Management, survey initiator
entities.quote: The assessment of archaeological resources within the proposed transmission system corridors is based primarily on a probabilistic sample survey initiated by BLM.
entities.stance_basis: initiated archaeological survey of proposed transmission corridors

entities.entity: San Diego Gas and Electric Company
entities.quote: San Diego Gas and Electric Company has provided joint frequency distributions of wind speed and direction by atmospheric stability class, based on the vertical temperature gradient, collected onsite during the period June 1, 1975, to May 31, 1976.
entities.stance_basis: applicant providing onsite meteorological data in support of project licensing

entities.entity: Bureau of Land Management Desert Planning Staff
entities.quote: The Bureau of Land Management Desert Planning Staff is required to complete a California Desert Management Plan by 1980. This study and plan could reveal uses that could compete with transmission lines for land. Completion of this plan is not a condition for granting permits for the Sundesert project.
entities.stance_basis: ongoing planning process that could compete with project land use but no explicit position stated

entities.role: federal water management agency / cooperating agency
entities.quote: It may be noted that the Bureau of Reclamation considers wells on the Palo Verde Mesa to be independent of the Colorado River system (ER, p. 2.4-31).
entities.stance_basis: provides factual determination about groundwater independence without taking a stance on the project

entities.quote: Southern California Edison (SCE) has done very preliminary studies on a possible nuclear generating station of about 1540 MW to be located a few kilometers east of Vidal Junction, California, and across the Colorado River from Parker, Arizona.
entities.stance_basis: SCE is a participant in related nuclear generation projects and plans to share a transmission line with SDG&E from the Sundesert plant

entities.entity: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power
entities.quote: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) has proposed to the California Energy Resources Conservation and Development Commission a plant site near the Vidal project described previously.
entities.stance_basis: LADWP is engaged in joint transmission corridor studies with SDG&E and SCE related to the Sundesert project

entities.quote: The Western Systems Coordinating Council (WSCC) makes transmission line routing projections to ensure adequate electric service interties between western utilities.
entities.stance_basis: provides transmission planning projections relevant to the project without taking an explicit stance

entities.quote: Although the Cibola National Wildlife Refuge is not a major water user, it is a Federal project operated by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
entities.stance_basis: identified as operator of ecologically sensitive refuge near the project site; no explicit stance on the project stated

entities.quote: In order to limit the impact of construction activities, the NRC staff will require that the applicant mark the boundaries of the construction work area in a conspicuous manner, instruct the work force to stay within the boundaries, and establish a formal program to monitor and enforce compliance.
entities.stance_basis: supports project conditionally, imposing multiple mitigation and monitoring requirements on the applicant

entities.quote: the NRC staff requires that the applicant determine whether the soils to be crossed have been treated and, if not, either avoid soils with perched water tables or arrange for breakage of the clay lenses prior to burial of the pipeline.
entities.stance_basis: conditions approval of pipeline on soil impact mitigation

entities.quote: the NRC staff requires that agricultural land be avoided unless the applicant can clearly demonstrate that the presence of the makeup water pipeline will not interfere with normal farming practices.
entities.stance_basis: conditions pipeline routing on demonstration of no farming interference

entities.quote: the NRC staff will require that dredging activities not be done during peak fish spawning periods (May and June).
entities.stance_basis: conditions construction on timing restriction for aquatic protection

entities.quote: the NRC staff will require that fencing of those areas or other appropriate means of protection be considered by the applicant and that the applicant's detailed plans be presented to the staff for review at least 90 days prior to initiation of any site preparation activities that may eventually be authorized by the NRC.
entities.stance_basis: conditions site preparation on archaeological protection plan review

entities.quote: The applicant will be required to present a detailed mitigation plan for NRC staff review and approval prior to issuance of a construction permit.
entities.stance_basis: conditions construction permit on housing mitigation plan submission

entities.quote: Each of the three proposed 500-kV transmission lines, according to SDG&E, would be designed with sufficient capacity to carry at least the entire output of the Sundesert Nuclear Plant to ensure reliability.
entities.stance_basis: applicant proposing and designing transmission infrastructure for the project

entities.quote: Southern California Edison also proposes to build a single-circuit 500-kV line between the proposed Sundesert plant and the Palo Verde Nuclear Generating Station under construction west of Phoenix, Arizona.
entities.stance_basis: proposing transmission infrastructure to connect to the Sundesert plant

entities.quote: This line will be discussed in a separate Environmental Statement being written by Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Arizona.
entities.stance_basis: preparing separate environmental review for related transmission line; no explicit support or opposition stated

entities.role: regulatory body governing overhead line construction
entities.quote: Construction, maintenance, and operation of all overhead electrical supply and communication lines in the state of California are governed by "Rules for Overhead Electric Line Construction," General Order No. 95, issued by the California Public Utilities Commission.
entities.stance_basis: sets regulatory standards applicable to project transmission lines; no explicit stance on project

entities.quote: The applicant's consultants, Imperial Valley College Museum (IVCM), have conducted extensive archaeological field work in the proposed siting area, as discussed in Sect. 2.5.3.1.2, and have concluded that the plant structures can be sited to avoid heavy concentrations of archaeological materials.
entities.stance_basis: concluded plant can be sited to avoid archaeological impacts, supporting project viability

entities.quote: The California Employment Development Department states that these are higher wages than are currently (1977) earned by most workers in the Palo Verde Valley.
entities.stance_basis: provides factual labor market information; no explicit stance on project approval

entities.quote: The California Department of Economic Development states that there is a higher than average level of unemployment currently among the contract construction trades in the eastern California region.
entities.stance_basis: provides labor market data relevant to project; no explicit stance on project approval

entities.quote: The NRC staff understands that the California Department of Economic Development, in conjunction with local unions and Palo Verde Community College, is studying the possibility of establishing training programs to upgrade skills of local workers.
entities.stance_basis: actively studying worker training programs in anticipation of project construction workforce needs

entities.quote: The NRC staff also recognizes that further mitigation measures may become necessary, depending on future implementation of advice received from the State Historic Preservation Officer (Appendix K), decisions by the Keeper of the National Register regarding the proposed nominations, and requirements that may be imposed by the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation.
entities.stance_basis: has provided advice on historic preservation; stance on project not yet determined

entities.quote: The NRC staff also recognizes that further mitigation measures may become necessary, depending on future implementation of advice received from the State Historic Preservation Officer (Appendix K), decisions by the Keeper of the National Register regarding the proposed nominations, and requirements that may be imposed by the Advisory Council on Historic Preservation.
entities.stance_basis: may impose requirements; no explicit stance on project stated

entities.entity: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP)
entities.quote: One or more additional 500-kV transmission lines might be needed to convey a portion of the Sundesert output to the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) and to the California Department of Water Resources (CDWR). Transmission line routes and final termini will be proposed by these departments in 1978.
entities.stance_basis: planning to receive power from Sundesert and proposing transmission routes

entities.quote: One or more additional 500-kV transmission lines might be needed to convey a portion of the Sundesert output to the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) and to the California Department of Water Resources (CDWR). Transmission line routes and final termini will be proposed by these departments in 1978.
entities.stance_basis: planning to receive power from Sundesert and proposing transmission routes

entities.entity: San Diego Gas and Electric Company (SDG&E)
entities.quote: SDG&E states that all the proposed 230-kV transmission lines would be in existing easements.
entities.stance_basis: applicant actively proposing and planning all transmission infrastructure for the project

entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that impacts to scenic/visual resources cannot be fully evaluated until final route selection is completed. The NRC staff feels, however, that, if the above mitigations are applied, if placement of the line within the corridor is done to minimize impacts, and if corridors are chosen to minimize impacts, no unacceptable impacts should occur.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds scenic/visual impacts acceptable only if specified mitigations and route minimization measures are applied

entities.quote: With the migitations proposed, the NRC staff feels that impacts to recreational resources should not be significant, with the exceptions of Anza-Borrego Desert State Park and Table Mountain.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds recreational impacts acceptable with mitigations, but flags severe impacts at Anza-Borrego and Table Mountain as exceptions

entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that, in most instances, archaeological impacts will be acceptable. However, a final conclusion cannot be made on archaeological impacts until agreement is reached concerning the final route.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff conditionally accepts archaeological impacts pending final route agreement and cultural resources management plan

entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that severe impacts may occur on cultural and historic resources only in a few instances. In most cases, transmission routing will have minor impacts on noted historic sites.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff notes mostly minor historic resource impacts but recommends careful attention during final routing

entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that the mitigations specified, including avoidance (see alternatives, Sect. 9.4), will result in there being no unacceptable impacts to biological habitats.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds biological habitat impacts acceptable only with specified mitigations and avoidance alternatives

entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that the mitigations specified, including avoidance (see alternatives, Sect. 9.4), will result in there being no unacceptable impacts to plant or animal species.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds species impacts acceptable only with specified mitigations and avoidance

entities.entity: California Department of Fish and Game
entities.quote: The Department of Fish and Game feels that this would not seriously interfere with this plan.
entities.stance_basis: Department of Fish and Game states the proposed line crossing the southeast corner of its proposed acquisition tract would not seriously interfere with its wildlife habitat acquisition plan

entities.quote: Therefore, a final determination will be required from the Bureau of Outdoor Recreation on the applicability of Section 6(f) of the Land and Water Conservation Fund Act to the proposed taking of land for the transmission line corridor.
entities.stance_basis: Bureau of Outdoor Recreation must make a final determination on Section 6(f) applicability — procedural role, no stance yet attributed

entities.quote: The Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) staff believes that the proposed revegetation plan would result in an unstable vegetative cover, which would not only require continual management to maintain, but which is potentially undesirable for aesthetic, biological, and soil conservative reasons.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff criticizes applicant's revegetation plan as unstable and undesirable, recommending a revised plan

entities.quote: The NRC staff does not consider this as a serious impact for the following reasons.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes entrainment of plankton is not a serious impact

entities.entity: California Department of Fish and Game
entities.quote: Some of these conclusions have been questioned by the California Fish and Game Department and the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (ER, p. 5.1-21).
entities.stance_basis: questioned applicant's drawdown conclusions regarding PVOD impacts

entities.quote: Some of these conclusions have been questioned by the California Fish and Game Department and the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service (ER, p. 5.1-21).
entities.stance_basis: questioned applicant's drawdown conclusions regarding PVOD impacts

entities.quote: The U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service will make all efforts possible to maintain the marsh in this area and prevent the concentration of flow in a typical U-shaped channel.
entities.stance_basis: committed to maintaining marsh habitat in the ORC area

entities.entity: San Diego Gas and Electric Company (SDG&E)
entities.quote: SDG&E has agreed to mitigate for possible fish and wildlife losses.
entities.stance_basis: agreed to mitigate fish and wildlife losses and pay for marsh habitat transformation

entities.entity: California Department of Fish and Game
entities.quote: California Department of Fish and Game and the U. S. Fish and Wildlife Service will do the contract specifications for the mitigation plan and will supervise the performance of the contract.
entities.stance_basis: agreed to develop and supervise mitigation plan for fish and wildlife losses

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: the NRC staff has concluded that there will be no measurable radiological impact on man from routine operation of the Sundesert plant.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes no measurable radiological impact from routine plant operation

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff finds that these combinations of thermal loadings and water consumption are acceptable, relative to the water use and thermal discharges of the proposed project.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds fuel cycle water/thermal impacts acceptable

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff finds that the direct and indirect consumption of electrical energy for fuel cycle operations is small and acceptable relative to the net power production of the proposed project.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds fuel cycle fossil fuel consumption acceptable

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that these impacts are acceptable, if mitigated as suggested by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM).
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds air facilities/airspace impacts acceptable with BLM-suggested mitigations

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that impacts to agriculture from the transmission lines will be acceptable with the mitigations proposed by BLM.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds agricultural impacts acceptable only with BLM-proposed mitigations

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that this small increase in the natural background dose will have no measureable radiological impact on man.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes fuel cycle radioactive effluents have no measurable radiological impact

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that these occupational doses will not have a significant environmental impact.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes occupational doses from fuel cycle are not significant

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff believes that earned income generated by the operation of the nuclear plant will add significantly to the long-range economic stability and diversification of the Palo Verde Valley.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff views plant operation as beneficial to local economy

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff recommends that the applicant establish training programs for local residents for technical operations-related jobs.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff recommends training programs as a condition for local employment benefit

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concurs with the applicant's estimates of housing demand.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concurs with applicant's housing demand estimates for operations phase

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concurs with the applicant's estimates and understands that the applicant has authorized a special educational facilities study to assist the Palo Verde Unified School District (PVUSD) in preparing cost and facilities estimates for both the construction and operating periods.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concurs with applicant's educational facilities estimates

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concurs with the applicant's position that, under present state law, the increased assessed valuation created by the nuclear plant construction should result in a sizable decrease in the PVUSD's annual tax rate for operations.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concurs with applicant's position on tax benefits to school district

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, project reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that operational impacts will be minimal.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes socioeconomic operational impacts will be minimal

entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that these impacts are acceptable, if mitigated as suggested by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM).
entities.stance_basis: BLM suggested mitigations for air facilities/airspace impacts, implying conditional acceptance

entities.role: member of the Atomic Safety and Licensing Board panel
entities.quote: Dr. Walter Jordan, a member of the Atomic Safety and Licensing Board panel, has raised concerns regarding the contribution and significance of radon as one of the radiological components of the environmental impacts of the uranium fuel cycle reflected in 10 CFR Part 51.20(e), Table S-3.
entities.stance_basis: Dr. Jordan raised concerns about radon's significance in fuel cycle radiological impacts

entities.role: federal agency proposing spent fuel storage policy
entities.quote: On October 18, 1977, the Department of Energy (DOE) announced a proposal whereby the Federal government will accept and take title to spent nuclear reactor fuel from utilities on payment of a one-time charge.
entities.stance_basis: DOE proposed federal acceptance of spent nuclear fuel, supporting nuclear plant operation

entities.quote: The proposed new policy, approved by President Carter, is a logical extension of the long-established Federal responsibility for permanent disposal of high-level wastes, in view of the present U.S. government position that reprocessing of civilian power reactor fuels be deferred indefinitely as a deterrent to proliferation.
entities.stance_basis: President Carter approved DOE policy for federal acceptance of spent nuclear fuel

entities.role: state agency, commenter on economic impacts
entities.quote: Officials at the California Department of Economic Development state that these salaries will be much higher than most other industrial salaries in the area in 1984.
entities.stance_basis: Officials noted plant salaries would be much higher than other local industrial salaries, implying economic benefit

entities.quote: Engineering studies are being conducted by SDG&E to predict, based on the limited data available, the worst-case AN levels of the proposed transmission lines.
entities.stance_basis: SDG&E is conducting studies to support the proposed transmission lines

entities.quote: On March 14, 1977, the Nuclear Regulatory Commission presented in the Federal Register (42 FR 13803) an interim rule regarding the environmental considerations of the uranium fuel cycle.
entities.stance_basis: NRC issued interim rule establishing environmental framework supporting nuclear fuel cycle operations

entities.quote: the NRC staff concludes that the participants' forecast constitutes a reasonable basis for planning new generating resources.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes participants' load forecasts and need for Sundesert Nuclear Plant are justified

entities.quote: the NRC staff concludes that the participants' have adequately accounted for the likely impacts of energy conservation in preparing their load forecasts
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff finds conservation adequately accounted for, supporting need determination for Sundesert

entities.quote: the NRC staff would conclude that a regional need will exist in all probability for new generating capacity in the amounts to be provided by the Sundesert Nuclear Plant but that SDG&L and LADWP shares of Sundesert are larger than needed to provide for an adequate reserve margin.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff supports regional need for Sundesert but flags SDG&E and LADWP shares as oversized relative to reserve margin criteria

entities.role: applicant / primary owner (50%) of Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.quote: San Diego Gas and Electric Company (SDG&E), the applicant, will be assumed to retain the remaining 50% (974 MWe) of Sundesert.
entities.stance_basis: SDG&E is the applicant and primary owner proposing the Sundesert Nuclear Plant

entities.quote: the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP), 20% (390 MWe)
entities.stance_basis: LADWP is a committed ownership participant in the Sundesert Nuclear Plant project

entities.quote: the California Department of Water Resources (CDWR), 15% (292 MWe)
entities.stance_basis: CDWR is a committed ownership participant in the Sundesert Nuclear Plant project

entities.quote: the City of Anaheim, 5% (97 MWe)
entities.stance_basis: City of Anaheim is a committed ownership participant in the Sundesert Nuclear Plant project

entities.quote: the City of Burbank, 2% (39 MWe)
entities.stance_basis: City of Burbank is a committed ownership participant in the Sundesert Nuclear Plant project

entities.role: project participant / 2% owner of Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.quote: the City of Glendale, 2% (39 MWe)
entities.stance_basis: City of Glendale is a committed ownership participant in the Sundesert Nuclear Plant project

entities.quote: the City of Pasadena, 2% (39 MWe)
entities.stance_basis: City of Pasadena is a committed ownership participant in the Sundesert Nuclear Plant project

entities.role: project participant / 4% owner of Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.quote: the City of Riverside, 4% (78 MWe)
entities.stance_basis: City of Riverside is a committed ownership participant in the Sundesert Nuclear Plant project

entities.role: consulted agency on Reactor Safety Study scope
entities.quote: The scope of the study has been discussed with the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA) and described in correspondence with EPA which has been placed in the NRC Public Document Room.
entities.stance_basis: EPA was consulted on the Reactor Safety Study scope but no position on the project is attributed

entities.entity: California Energy Resources Conservation and Development Commission
entities.role: provider of common forecasting methodology used by participants
entities.quote: With the exception of Burbank and CDWR, each of the participants based its forecasting methodology on the California Energy Resources Conservation and Development Commission's (ERCDC's) common forecasting methodology developed by Economic Sciences Corporation.
entities.stance_basis: ERCDC developed the forecasting methodology adopted by participants but no direct stance on the project is attributed

entities.role: developer of independent econometric model used by NRC staff
entities.quote: an independent regional forecast was prepared by the NRC staff using an econometric electrical energy demand model developed at Oak Ridge National Laboratory (ORNL).
entities.stance_basis: ORNL provided the independent forecasting model; no stance on the project itself is attributed

entities.role: power pool setting minimum reserve margin criteria applicable to SDG&E
entities.quote: The CPP mandates a minimum reserve margin of 10% over forecasted peak demand prior to deducting scheduled maintenance and 5% after deducting scheduled maintenance.
entities.stance_basis: CPP sets reserve criteria used in need analysis but no stance on Sundesert project is attributed

entities.role: regional reliability council setting reserve margin criteria for member systems
entities.quote: The WSCC has recommended minimum reserve margin criteria for its member systems that follow along similar lines.
entities.stance_basis: WSCC provides reserve criteria used in need analysis but no stance on Sundesert project is attributed

entities.entity: California Energy Resources Conservation and Development Commission (ERCDC)
entities.role: state energy regulatory commission, reviewer of site selection process
entities.quote: As a result of the California Energy Resources Conservation and Development Commission's (ERCDC's) review of the site selection process, the applicant's consultant conducted an additional review of the Camp Pendleton Marine Corps Base.
entities.stance_basis: triggered additional site review of Camp Pendleton without taking a position on project approval

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, EIS author/reviewer
entities.quote: the staff concludes that development of a site at Camp Pendleton is not a viable option if a nuclear plant is to be constructed on the schedule established for the Sundesert project.
entities.stance_basis: concludes Camp Pendleton not viable due to delays and uncertainties, implying Blythe site preferred under project schedule constraints

entities.role: state agency commenter on coastal resources
entities.quote: Comments received from the Resources Agency of California indicate that the Camp Pendleton area contains a number of identified coastal resources that must also be considered in evaluating a site at that location.
entities.stance_basis: identified coastal resource constraints at Camp Pendleton without explicitly opposing or supporting the project

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, economic and environmental analyst
entities.quote: the NRC staff concludes that no significant reduction in the overall environmental impact of station construction and operation would be gained by selection of the Blythe coal or Parker Valley nuclear alternatives.
entities.stance_basis: concludes Blythe nuclear option is superior to coal and Parker Valley alternatives

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, economic analyst
entities.quote: the NRC staff concludes that the coal alternative is not likely to present any significant economic advantage over the proposed Sundesert Nuclear Plant.
entities.stance_basis: economic analysis favors nuclear over coal alternative

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, site selection reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff further concludes from its analysis of the alternative sites identified by the applicant and site-plant alternatives that none is obviously superior to the Blythe nuclear option for a plant to be constructed by the mid 1980s.
entities.stance_basis: concludes Blythe nuclear option is the best candidate site-plant alternative

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, cooling system reviewer
entities.quote: The NRC staff agrees with the applicant that the multicell mechanical-draft towers in either a rectangular or circular layout are a logical choice for the Sundesert plant.
entities.stance_basis: agrees with applicant's selection of multicell mechanical-draft cooling towers as reference design

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, cooling system reviewer
entities.quote: the NRC staff does not take issue with the applicant's selection of the multicell mechanical-draft towers as the reference design.
entities.stance_basis: does not challenge applicant's selection of multicell towers over single-fan concept despite some advantages of the latter

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, wet-dry cooling tower analyst
entities.quote: The NRC staff believes that the additional investment required for wet-dry towers would be questionable purely on the basis of the present-day cost of water.
entities.stance_basis: questions economic justification for wet-dry towers without definitively opposing or endorsing them

entities.quote: Multicell mechanical-draft cooling towers are proposed by the applicant as the most suitable method for waste heat dissipation at the Sundesert Nuclear Plant.
entities.stance_basis: proposes multicell mechanical-draft cooling towers as preferred design for the Sundesert plant

entities.role: scientific body, author of report to U.S. Senate on sulfur emissions health effects
entities.quote: They are described by the National Academy of Sciences (NAS) in a recent report to the U.S. Senate.
entities.stance_basis: described tentative cause-effect relationships for sulfur emissions without taking a position on the Sundesert project

entities.role: research laboratory, author of health effects assessments for coal fuel cycle
entities.quote: The most recent and detailed assessments of health effects of the coal fuel cycle have been prepared by the Brookhaven and Argonne National Laboratories.
entities.stance_basis: provided health effects data used in comparative analysis without taking a project stance

entities.role: research laboratory, author of health effects assessments for coal fuel cycle
entities.quote: The most recent and detailed assessments of health effects of the coal fuel cycle have been prepared by the Brookhaven and Argonne National Laboratories.
entities.stance_basis: provided health effects data used in comparative analysis without taking a project stance

entities.entity: National Research Council Committee on Nuclear and Alternative Energy Systems
entities.role: scientific advisory committee conducting comprehensive energy alternatives assessment
entities.quote: A more comprehensive assessment of these two alternatives and others is anticipated late in 1977 from the National Research Council Committee on Nuclear and Alternative Energy Systems.
entities.stance_basis: anticipated to provide further analysis; no position on Sundesert project attributed

entities.role: federal regulatory agency, author of CHESS study and New Source Performance Standards
entities.quote: The EPA has estimated radiation doses from coal and nuclear plants of early designs and reached similar conclusions.
entities.stance_basis: provided comparative radiation dose estimates without taking a position on the Sundesert project

entities.role: state coastal regulatory agency whose approval would be needed for Camp Pendleton cooling water infrastructure
entities.quote: the additional uncertainty of receiving approval of the California Coastal Commission for the installation of cooling water intake and discharge pipes to the ocean
entities.stance_basis: identified as a source of regulatory uncertainty for Camp Pendleton option; no explicit position attributed

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, EIS author/evaluator
entities.quote: The NRC staff concludes that either route would be acceptable. The alternative proposed by BLM (I-2) would result in somewhat greater visual impacts than the proposed route but would significantly reduce impacts to recreation, wilderness, and archaeological resources. However, visual impact to Interstate 10 travellers would be less than for the SDG&E alternative. Overall, the BLM alternative I-2 appears to have fewer significant impacts associated with it and represents the NRC staff's preference.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff expresses preferences and acceptability determinations for multiple corridor alternatives throughout section 9.4.7

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, EIS author/evaluator
entities.quote: Consequently, the NRC staff does not consider this an acceptable alternative.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff rejects Links 92b as an acceptable alternative because it passes through a candidate wilderness area in San Bernardino National Forest

entities.quote: However, the Department of the Interior does not foreclose the adoption of this alternative.
entities.stance_basis: Department of the Interior keeps open the option of Links 92b alternative that NRC staff rejected

entities.role: Bureau of Land Management, cooperating agency/commenter
entities.quote: The NRC staff concurs with BLM and SDG&E that the proposed corridor poses fewer significant impacts than the alternative.
entities.stance_basis: BLM concurs that the proposed Campo to Miguel corridor has fewer significant impacts than the alternative

entities.role: San Diego Gas and Electric Company, project applicant
entities.quote: The NRC staff concurs with BLM and SDG&E that the proposed corridor poses fewer significant impacts than the alternative.
entities.stance_basis: SDG&E concurs that the proposed Campo to Miguel corridor has fewer significant impacts than the alternative

entities.entity: Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP)
entities.role: participant in Sundesert project, 20% power share
entities.quote: Both departments have stated a preference for owning their transmission lines rather than wheeling power over lines owned by Southern California Edison (SCE).
entities.stance_basis: LADWP prefers owning its own transmission lines rather than wheeling power over SCE lines

entities.role: participant in Sundesert project, 16% power share
entities.quote: Both departments have stated a preference for owning their transmission lines rather than wheeling power over lines owned by Southern California Edison (SCE).
entities.stance_basis: CDWR prefers owning its own transmission lines rather than wheeling power over SCE lines

entities.role: SDG&E-sponsored local committee, commenter on routing alternatives
entities.quote: SDG&E has sponsored the Imperial Valley Committee with the understanding that the applicant will seriously consider any routing alternatives in Imperial County proposed by this committee to avoid agricultural lands.
entities.stance_basis: Imperial Valley Committee proposes routing alternatives in Imperial County to avoid agricultural lands

entities.role: witness, Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.quote: Prepared Testimony of Gordon W. Hoyt for the Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on the Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.stance_basis: Listed as reference for testimony at California Energy Resources and Development Commission hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant

entities.role: City of Riverside witness, Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.quote: Prepared Testimony of Donald Campbell, City of Riverside, for Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on the Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.stance_basis: Listed as reference for testimony at California Energy Resources and Development Commission hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant

entities.role: witness, Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.quote: Prepared Testimony of Ronald V. Stassi for the Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on the Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.stance_basis: Listed as reference for testimony at California Energy Resources and Development Commission hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant

entities.role: witness, Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.quote: Prepared Testimony of Evans E. Hughes for the Notice of Intent Adjudicatory Hearings on the Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.stance_basis: Listed as reference for testimony at California Energy Resources and Development Commission hearings on Sundesert Nuclear Plant

entities.role: witness on fuel cell alternative, California Energy Resources and Development Commission hearings
entities.quote: Testimony of L. Kirk Hall on the Fuel Cell Alternative to the Sundesert Nuclear Plant
entities.stance_basis: Listed as reference for testimony on fuel cell alternative to Sundesert Nuclear Plant

entities.entity: California Department of Fish and Game
entities.role: state wildlife agency, referenced in impact analysis
entities.quote: If this tract were acquired for a wildlife refuge, the California Department of Fish and Game would construct a lake near the southwest corner.
entities.stance_basis: Referenced as planning a lake near the southwest corner of a proposed wildlife development that would be affected by Alternative I-9

entities.entity: San Diego Gas and Electric Company
entities.quote: The applicant has made an initial response to the proposal (ER, p. Q.3-1) indicating its willingness to consider such an alternative, but no detailed study has been performed.
entities.stance_basis: SDG&E indicates willingness to consider placing all 500-kV lines along the northern corridor but has not performed detailed study

entities.quote: This alternative would avoid bisecting the settlement of New Mirage from the community of Ocotillo. Ocotillo residents consider New Mirage a part of Ocotillo.
entities.stance_basis: Ocotillo residents consider New Mirage part of their community, relevant to routing impact assessment

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, EIS author/reviewer
entities.quote: the NRC staff concludes that there will be no measureable radiological impact on man from routine operation of the Sundesert plant.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes radiological impacts are within acceptable limits and primary benefit of electrical energy outweighs environmental and economic costs

entities.role: Nuclear Regulatory Commission staff, EIS author/reviewer
entities.quote: the NRC staff concludes that the primary benefit of the increased availability of electrical energy outweighs the environmental and economic costs of the plant.
entities.stance_basis: NRC staff concludes benefits outweigh costs and no alternative would significantly improve the balance

entities.entity: San Diego Gas and Electric Company (SDG&E)
entities.quote: Surface clearing for the 500-kV transmission towers, access roads, pulling sites, and crane pads will likely exceed the 443.2 ha (1108 acres) estimated by San Diego Gas and Electric Company (SDG&E).
entities.stance_basis: SDG&E submitted surface clearing calculations as part of project application, implying support for the proposed project

entities.role: federal agency responsible for uranium resource assessment
entities.quote: ERDA assessment of uranium resources indicates that currently estimated $30 ore reserves byproduct and probable potential resources consist of 1.9 million tons U3O8.
entities.stance_basis: ERDA provides resource assessments and programs to assure uranium supply adequacy without taking a position on the Sundesert project itself

entities.role: private organization identifying sensitive plant species
entities.quote: Designations may have been made by the Director, U.S. Department of the Interior, Fish and Wildlife Service, the Director, California Department of Fish and Game, or by various organizations which lack official status, such as the California Native Plant Society and the National Audubon Society.
entities.stance_basis: California Native Plant Society identified over 700 sensitive plant species relevant to the project area, informing environmental review

entities.quote: Designations may have been made by the Director, U.S. Department of the Interior, Fish and Wildlife Service, the Director, California Department of Fish and Game, or by various organizations which lack official status, such as the California Native Plant Society and the National Audubon Society.
entities.stance_basis: National Audubon Society identified sensitive species relevant to the project area, informing environmental review

entities.quote: To date, only animal species have been officially listed as endangered or threatened by the U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service.
entities.stance_basis: U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service provided official species listings used in environmental impact assessment of the project

entities.entity: California Department of Fish and Game
entities.quote: Designations may have been made by the Director, U.S. Department of the Interior, Fish and Wildlife Service, the Director, California Department of Fish and Game, or by various organizations which lack official status, such as the California Native Plant Society and the National Audubon Society.
entities.stance_basis: California Department of Fish and Game provided official state species listings used in environmental impact assessment of the project

entities.quote: The Forest Service is extremely concerned that no alternative is proposed for this area.
entities.stance_basis: expressed extreme concern that no alternative is proposed for the San Bernardino Forest area affected by the northern 500-kV corridor

entities.quote: There would be high impacts in the short and long term on BLM's development of a visitor services facility at Ocotillo. Opportunities for environmental education and interpretive activities associated with this facility would be severely restricted.
entities.stance_basis: BLM's planned visitor services facility at Ocotillo would be severely restricted by the southern 500-kV corridor, indicating concern about project impacts on BLM programs
